action: Emerges from the tent area toward the firepit, drawn by voices and laughter.
thought: She's laughing. That's not from before.

action: Tends the fire at midday, warm and present as Marcus and Jade arrive at the firepit.
thought: They're both here, both okay. That's enough.

action: Drifts to the firepit and settles beside Marcus, visibly relaxing as the morning's weight dissolves.
thought: He's okay. We're okay. I can feel it.

action: Sits at the beach edge, watching the distant firepit and trying to fill the void of time.
thought: They're together. I don't belong there.

action: Drifts closer to the firepit, drawn by the warmth and the ease in human voices.
thought: They're calm now. I can rest here.

description: When pressed on his closed-off tone, Marcus jokes—a rare crack—and the three choose to sit in silence together while the puppy draws close.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey, you're up. How's the tent treating you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He says fine about everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You do, actually. You feeling okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Why would I not be?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: After last night... and this morning...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What about it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on. You two had this whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I asked him if I hurt him, and—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But you're fine now, right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Obviously.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not fine. Your voice gets like that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Closed. You sound closed off.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not intentional.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. That's why I noticed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, we're here now. All three of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Is that what was supposed to happen?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't know what's supposed to happen.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe nothing dramatic. Maybe we just sit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sitting. How ambitious.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did you just make a joke?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Apparently.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Should we document this? Marcus being funny?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better things to do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sitting. Not talking if possible.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's exactly what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we're doing it together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The pup's come closer. He's less scared now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He can feel when things shift in us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're better. We're better.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We are.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: This is good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll take it.

action: Sits by the fire with the group, trading dry barbs with Ryan while watching Jade laugh.
thought: She laughs at my jokes. That's still surprising.

action: Settles by the fire with everyone gathered, warm and present, welcoming Judy into the circle.
thought: Everyone's here. This is what matters.

action: Laughs and teases between Marcus and Ryan, fully at ease, lighter than she's been in days.
thought: Both of them, easy together. It's good.

action: Steps out of the shadows and finally sits with the group at the fire, hesitant but present.
thought: They want me here. Actually want me.

action: Leans against Ryan by the fire, then walks slowly over to Judy and presses against her leg.
thought: She's safe. They're all safe.

description: Judy breaks her silence to ask if she belongs, and the answer comes not just in words but in the stray choosing her leg—a vote the whole group agrees with.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus, I've been thinking about your tent.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Don't.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's room in the house. You'd have actual walls.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tent's fine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He'll never leave that tent, Ryan. You know that by now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why not give it a try?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're so persistent about building things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's what happens when you can't let anything be.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I built us shelter. Real shelter. I'm proud of it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You did great. But maybe you love the building more than the houses.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He likes being in control.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's nothing wrong with wanting things solid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Nothing. Except you can't stop.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He has a point though. You're always tense.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Someone has to be.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [wanders over and leans against Ryan's leg, seeking warmth]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. Even the pup knows where safety is.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup is just hungry.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Aren't we all looking for something?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Some more than others.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is it... can I sit with you all? I don't want to intrude.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, yes. Come sit.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Please. We want you here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sit.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I like being with you all. More than anything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You belong here. You know that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: More than you think.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even when I'm just quiet and scared?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Especially then.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not a burden.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're part of this. Of us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't always feel like I am.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we're not saying it enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're saying it now.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [stands and walks to Judy, pressing against her leg with gentle trust]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. The pup is voting.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's his yes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He knows.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: This is good. All of us, like this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Really good.

action: He settles by the firepit left side, teasing Jade with a dry edge that surprises even himself; a real smile lingers after, unguarded.
thought: Laughing with her feels right now.

action: He welcomes Judy to the fire with genuine warmth, stroking the stray pup's ear while tending the flames—holding space for everyone, comfortable being the warmth.
thought: This is what it feels like to matter.

action: She sits at the fire, teasing Marcus while welcoming Judy—the bridge between all of them, easy and radiant, her own lightness generous.
thought: Everyone's here and safe. That matters.

action: She settles trembling at the fire, her hand finally steady enough to stroke the pup who chose her, tears that don't fall just behind her eyes.
thought: They wanted me here. They still do.

action: The pup curls against Judy's hip, sensing her fear and answering with quiet presence—safe now, trusted, the warmth of belonging at last.
thought: She's scared but safe. I'll stay here.

description: Three voices answer Judy's doubt: Ryan insists they want her, Marcus affirms she belongs, Jade invites her to sit—an unmistakable yes that she's one of them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, for someone who claims to hate mornings, you sure wake up early to complain about them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't hate mornings. I hate Ryan loudly making fire at dawn.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every stick. Narrated individually.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's not fair. I'm methodical, not loud.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You announced each piece of kindling like it was a prize.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: With commentary.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know what? Fine. But someone has to tend the fire, and clearly the two of you are content to sit in darkness.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not content. We're just quiet.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even the pup understands my value.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The pup tolerates you. Barely.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still learning who's safe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well, you both smiled today. I saw real smiles.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus smiled?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not by choice.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That makes it matter more.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I don't want to interrupt. I just... can I sit?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come here. Sit with us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes. Please. We want you here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You do belong here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh... you want me too.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He knows who's good.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We all know.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Stop waiting for us to change our minds. We won't.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm trying to believe that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You can. And we're here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're staying.

action: Emerges from his tent as the sun breaks over the eastern ridge, stretching and pausing to listen to the camp stirring awake.
thought: She laughs at my jokes. Still surprising.

action: Crouches by the fire pit with fresh wood, watching the flames catch and strengthen in the early morning light.
thought: She should be awake soon. Wanting her to be.

action: Wakes in her hammock to golden light filtering through the palms, lies still for a moment listening to the camp, then pushes herself up.
thought: I want to know what I'm actually feeling.

action: Steps out onto the house porch into the early light, one hand on the wooden post, breathing in the salt air with something like peace.
thought: They chose me. They want me here.

action: Stretches from its sleeping spot in the shade near the palms, tail lifting as it scents the morning breeze and hears the camp waking.
thought: Ryan's here. Safe here. Everything safe.

description: Before the island fully wakes, Jade and Ryan speak what's been building: she's choosing him and staying, he's terrified of losing her, and both finally claim it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're up early.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Hammock gets too hot once the sun hits it. You sleep at all?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Not much. I was up thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: About yesterday. About all of us actually fitting together like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We do fit now. That part changed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. I felt lighter. Did you feel it too?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Something shifted, yeah. Everything felt different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think it's because I stopped pretending I don't care about... about certain people.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Certain people. That's one way to put it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Specific people. You know who I mean.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I think I do. I've been thinking about you too, by the way.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. And about Marcus, but it's different. He makes me laugh—that's new.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That is new. He's been different since you got here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He has. But you were different from the start.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Different how?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You looked at me like I mattered. Like I wasn't just passing through.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You do matter. You're not passing through.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know that now. I was drifting before. Didn't know what I wanted.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now I want to stay. Want to be here with all of you. Especially with you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That means a lot to me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know it does. That's why I'm saying it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was afraid you might leave.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not leaving. Not unless you want me to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't want that. I really don't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What are you thinking?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That I'm glad. That this is real. That you're choosing to be here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I am choosing. I'm choosing this. I'm choosing you.

action: Sits at the front of his tent, watching the firepit where Ryan and Jade sit close together, feeling the distance widen.
thought: She's choosing him. I already knew.

action: Sits by the fire at midday, drawing Jade close and listening as she admits what she fears most, his hand steady in hers.
thought: She needs me. She actually needs me.

action: Sits close to Ryan by the fire at midday, speaking her fear aloud for the first time and feeling his hand ground her.
thought: He's not leaving. He won't leave.

action: Gathers firewood from the pile at the camp's edge, working steadily and trying not to watch the three of them at the fire.
thought: They have something I need.

action: Rests against Ryan's leg by the fire, sensing the warmth between them and content to belong to this moment.
thought: Safe. All of us safe here.

description: Jade's promise to stay present—to hold his hand and stop thinking about leaving—finally stops her from running.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're quiet. Everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. Just thinking. About us. About this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What about it? You're making me nervous.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: In a good way or bad way?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Depends on what you're about to tell me, I guess.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not used to being wanted like this. Not like you want me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that a bad thing?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's scary. Because I might lose it. Lose you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not losing me. I'm right here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you could leave. Get bored. Find someone else.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I won't. I know myself. I don't fall halfway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's what scares me more. How much you mean it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why does that scare you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because if you leave, it will actually destroy me. Not like before.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So don't think like that. Think like I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I want to. I really do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then do. Be here with me like you're choosing me too.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I am. I just said that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, you're here with me but you're still ready to run. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe. That's how I've always survived.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not surviving anymore. You're living. With me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. And it feels real. That's the problem.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Real is good. Real is what I want.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Real means I could get hurt.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. But it also means this matters. What we have right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It does. More than I know what to do with.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then just hold my hand. Don't think about leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Promise?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Promise. Right now, I promise.

action: Stands at the tent edge as evening falls, watching the firelight glow in the distance where they sit together.
thought: She's choosing him. Still choosing him.

action: Tends the fire in the gathering darkness, the flames painting his face warm as he waits for her.
thought: She'll come back. She always does.

action: Walks slowly down from the path toward the fire, settling close beside Ryan as evening gathers.
thought: I can do this. I can stay.

action: Sits on the house porch in the growing darkness, watching the two figures by the fire with longing.
thought: They have something. They found something.

action: Settles between Ryan and Jade by the fire, belly warm, ears relaxed, completely at peace.
thought: Safe. Everyone safe here now.

description: Against her instinct to flee, Jade chooses to stay—not because she's suddenly brave, but because Ryan's unwavering presence makes running feel impossible.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're quiet tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just thinking. About you. About us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What did you decide?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That I'm terrified. And that I want to stay anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then stay. Please.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's not that simple.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because I'm the person who runs. What if I do that to you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You won't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How can you be so sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because I know myself, and I know that if you're here, I'm not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Even when I'm hard to be around?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Especially then.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't want to hurt you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not hurting me by being here. You could only hurt me by leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But what if that's what happens?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then it happens. And I'll have loved you anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That seems reckless.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe it is. But I don't want to be careful with you anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't know if I can give you what you need.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You already are. You're here. That's everything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How are you this sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because I see you. The real you, not who you think you should be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That terrifies me more than anything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. But you don't have to be scared alone now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You promise?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Every single day if you need me to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if I wake up panicked?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then I'll be there.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, I believe you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'm choosing this. Choosing you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all I needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For being patient. For staying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Always.

action: He approaches the fire to add wood as Ryan and Jade sit close; he kneels beside the flames and joins their conversation, speaking harder truths than he usually allows.
thought: She's beautiful when she's not afraid. He sees that too.

action: He sits at the fire with Jade beside him, their hands finding each other in the darkness, speaking softly through the night as the warmth settles between them.
thought: She's choosing me. She's really choosing me.

action: She drifts down to the fire as night deepens, settles against Ryan's side, and lets herself be seen by both him and Marcus at once—unguarded and real.
thought: I don't have to be afraid anymore. That's the real fear.

action: She stands on the house porch watching the fire below, seeing them gathered in the warmth; her hands grip the railing until her knuckles pale.
thought: I don't belong in that circle. I never will.

action: The pup presses close to the humans at the fire, its large head resting against Jade's leg, tail thumping steadily in the firelight as it feels all of them here, together.
thought: Safe. Everybody here. All safe.

description: Jade voices what she sees in both men—Marcus's careful kindness, Ryan's honest hope—and asks them to stay, choosing belief over fear.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back down. I wasn't sure you would.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep. The house is too still when it's dark. Even knowing you're near, it's...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're still scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not of you. Never of you. Of what it means that I'm not scared anymore.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then maybe it's time to stop being scared. I'll be here through all of it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire's burning low.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was going to add wood in a minute. You okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll do it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're different. Less trapped-looking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that... a compliment?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's an observation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus is secretly a poet. Compliments in the form of gentle insults.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not poetry.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, he's right. You are like that. You say difficult things as kindly as you can.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Someone has to say true things. Everyone else just says what feels good.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I say true things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You say hopeful things. Different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe. But hope is true too, if you're willing to believe it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starting to believe it. That's the scary part.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Belief is always scary. It means you have something to lose.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the most philosophical thing I've heard you say.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm full of surprises.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're full of surprises but you never surprise me with meanness. I think that's why I trust you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You really are. I thought I had you figured out on the first day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now I think you're the most careful person here. Like you're trying not to break anything—not the island, not the camp, not us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Hard to break things that are already broken.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Nothing here is broken, mate.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not broken either. Just... careful. I respect that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You should get some sleep. You look exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So do you. But you'll stay awake anyway, watching the fire.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Someone has to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We could take shifts. I can keep an eye on it later.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stay. Both of you. Just a little longer. Please.

action: He wakes to quiet tent light, gathers an armload of firewood, and makes his way toward the firepit.
thought: She's there. I can feel it.

action: He tends the coals of the firepit, adding wood with the care of someone who barely slept, watching the warmth build.
thought: She's here with me. This is real.

action: She drifts from the hammock toward the firepit, drawn by the light and the gathering, moving without hurry.
thought: Morning light and warmth. They're both here.

action: She stands on the house porch with a cup of water, watching the three at the fire in the distance, her hand moving to her belly.
thought: They have something I can't reach.

action: The pup pads soft-footed toward the gathered warmth of the firepit, tail cautious but rising, settling between the seated figures.
thought: Safe. All here. All warm.

description: They decide against splitting up: instead of separate hunts, all three pack supplies and move down the coast together, the pup trailing, intent on fishing the tide.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's still strong. Could've gone out with how hard we slept.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You kept checking it in the dark, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Can't help it. Just... habit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire doesn't keep itself.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Never said it did, mate.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should eat while we have the warmth. The food is running low.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Two days left if we're careful. We need to fish properly today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I can set traps down the coast. Need to move anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if all three of us went together? The ocean's good if the tide is right.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That work? All together?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine. Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He knows something's different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He knows we're okay. That's all he needs.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus, wait—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Nothing. Just... thank you. For staying. For being here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was here. Needed to be, actually.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We all were. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water first. Then we pack for fishing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I want to come with you. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then let's move before the heat gets unbearable.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The fire's in good shape. We can leave it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Pup's ready. He wants to move.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll find enough out there. I know it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We always do. When we're together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then let's go. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come on, then.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You coming, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Right behind you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good.

action: Tends the fire with quiet focus, watching how easily Ryan and Jade fit together while the stray settles between them.
thought: I'm still here. That's enough.

action: Sits by the fire at midday with Jade beside him, absently petting the stray while Marcus brings firewood and joins the warmth.
thought: We have something real here. All of us.

action: Settles beside Ryan at the firepit, feeling the easy warmth of his presence and noticing how Marcus and the stray complete the circle.
thought: This is peace. Didn't expect it here.

action: Sits alone on the house porch, watching the four gathered at the firepit—Ryan, Jade, Marcus, and the stray—finding warmth in a circle she cannot reach.
thought: They have each other. I'm still outside.

action: Pads between the three at the firepit, settling against Ryan's warmth, then rises and trots toward the porch to check on Judy before returning to the group.
thought: Safe here. All safe. But... she's alone.

description: As the pup's faith in safety grows, Judy's distance becomes clear—and the three resolve to show her she belongs.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's holding steady. We're good for a few hours.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I love mornings like this—everything just... settled.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than yesterday's panic.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Look who's braver now. Having a good day, little one?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's stronger. I can feel it—like it finally believes it's safe here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still flinches when the wind picks up.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's okay. So do I, sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all learning, I guess.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're doing good with it. The dog, I mean.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah? I was just... there when it needed someone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's exactly what it needed. That's what we all need—to matter to someone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy's been quiet today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She's watching. Figuring out where she fits.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you think she's okay?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know. This morning she seemed... separate. Like she was already gone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She is separate. But that doesn't mean she'll always be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should go to her. Show her she belongs with us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You think she'd want that after this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Won't know till you ask. But waiting sure isn't helping.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look, even the dog knows.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: At least something's sure of the welcome.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are too. You just haven't believed it yet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, yeah. After this—we'll go find her.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good. Come on.

action: Returned from fishing and helped clean the catch by firelight, watching Jade draw Judy quietly into the group.
thought: She understood something we're all still learning.

action: Tended the fire for cooking while the pup and Jade worked to bring Judy back in without fuss.
thought: Maybe we broke something today. She's fixing it.

action: Left the fishing group's comfort to find Judy waiting alone and sat with her as evening deepened.
thought: She was drowning and I didn't see it.

action: Waited on the porch all day until the pup sensed it and Jade came, finally breaking the isolation.
thought: Maybe there's room. Maybe I'm not alone.

action: Left the fishing trio mid-evening and went straight to Judy, pressing close in the growing dusk.
thought: Her sadness called me. I went. That's how.

description: The pup, sensing Judy's loneliness, breaks from the fishing trio to find her on the porch, and Jade understands immediately.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You know we were fishing for dinner tomorrow too, right? Not just today.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I know. I guess I just... I'm tired all the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come sit with me by the fire?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You three were having a good day together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were. But the day's not over yet.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Ryan and you, you're so easy together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's steady. But that doesn't mean...

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: And Marcus, he actually talked to you. He hardly talks to anyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He talks to all of us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Not like that. Not with that... I don't know. Ease.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come sit. Please.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You feel bad for me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I didn't know you were alone until now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm always alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not. See?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The pup just wants food.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's been eating fish all day. He wants you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of what?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: That there's no room for me here. That I'll always be watching from outside.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There's room. Come sit with me. With us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Why would you want that?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you're here. And because I didn't realize you were hurting.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm not angry at you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But I'm still here now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's almost ready for dinner.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We got enough for days.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy's going to help us eat it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: If you want me to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course we want you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're part of this.

action: He arrives at the porch as darkness settles, his simple presence completing the circle without need for words.
thought: The circle's whole. I'm part of it.

action: He settles beside Jade, making sure everyone is warm, his hand resting near hers on the bench.
thought: She's safe. We all are now.

action: She keeps herself close to Judy, her body angled toward her, one hand on her knee.
thought: I see her now. That changes everything.

action: She leans into the warmth around her, the stray's weight against her leg like an anchor.
thought: Maybe I don't have to be alone.

action: She presses against Judy's leg, sighing with the deep contentment of a pup who found her person.
thought: She's safe. The pack is whole.

description: On the porch as night falls, the group gathers—the stray pressed against Judy, who finally sits in the center, Jade beside her, Ryan and Marcus completing a circle that couldn't hold without her.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Everyone okay? Warm enough?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah, just sitting. It's nice.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I... can I stay? Out here with you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course you can. You always could.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She knows that now, I think.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The pup knows it. Come here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: She really came back to me. I wasn't sure if...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: She knows you're one of us. So do we.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup doesn't lie about these things.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Neither do we. Not tonight anyway.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What changed? This morning I was...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You were fine. It was us that was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We saw it too late. But we're seeing it now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we're not looking away again.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I kept waiting for you to forget about me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, you're not the kind of person we forget.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You matter. You're part of this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The circle doesn't work without you in it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even though I'm a lot? Even though I'm...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even because. You're exactly who we need.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stop questioning it. Just be here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's all there is.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Should we think about heading in? It's getting late.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not yet. Let's stay like this.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Yeah. Like this. Please.

action: Wakes in the tent to grey-blue light and listens to the camp stirring without pulling away.
thought: The circle is real. I belong in it.

action: Feeds the fire with wood from the pile, moving slowly and humming something without thinking.
thought: Everyone's here. Everything feels right this morning.

action: Walks back from the beach with an armful of driftwood, barefoot and unhurried, noticing Marcus is awake.
thought: I'm not thinking. That's when I'm most honest.

action: Opens her eyes slowly in the house, checks that Ryan is still at the fire, that it's real.
thought: Maybe I belong here now. Maybe it stays.

action: Wakes in the palm shade, remembers the warmth of last night's circle, stretches and moves toward the fire.
thought: Everyone is there. I'm safe with them all.

description: At the firepit at dawn, Jade brings driftwood and sits beside Marcus; for the first time, he doesn't pull away from her.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You both up early?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The beach called. I needed to walk down there.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The light is good on the rocks at this time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You've noticed that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Hard not to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Should we fish today? Food's gone and the tide's right for it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I could go. Marcus knows the deep pools better than I do.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The inlet's better than the rocks. More fish there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You two go fishing. I'll watch the fire and check on Judy if she wakes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup will follow us. It always does.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It likes the water now. Do you remember when it was scared of the waves?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Three days ago it wouldn't come near the fire. Now it barely leaves your side.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Animals settle fast if they feel safe. People are slower about it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So have you. Settled, I mean.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I haven't settled. I've just... stayed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the same thing, though. Staying is how you settle.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come on, then. Before the sun gets too high.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Alright.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Should we bring anything? Nets, or something to carry fish in?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We'll find what we need when we get there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Go on. I've got this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Keep the fire up. The wood's right there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. Don't worry about it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Ready?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Let's go.

action: Gathers the fishing net with methodical purpose and heads toward the tide pools without hesitation.
thought: They needed me. I'm going.

action: Counts the empty food stores and looks up, relief crossing his face as he spots Marcus already heading toward the beach.
thought: No food. Marcus knows. We can fix this.

action: Drifts toward the shoreline, drawn by a feeling she can't name, and watches the two men prepare.
thought: Something's pulling me toward the water.

action: Sits on the house porch with a water glass, watching the three of them gather gear and head toward the beach.
thought: They're doing something. Should I go?

action: Trots after the humans toward the beach with tail raised high, eager to be part of whatever they're doing.
thought: They go. I go. We stay together.

description: At midday, with the stores empty, Marcus and Jade both walk toward the beach at the same moment—drawn by Ryan's quiet crisis and by something neither of them can quite name.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We have no food. Zero. We need fish, like, immediately.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Already on it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You knew?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I checked this morning. The stores were low.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I smell the ocean. Is something happening?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade, the ocean's what's happening. We need to fish.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Oh. Then I definitely have to come.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know, but you both look like you need someone to make this fun.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We don't need—okay, actually, yes, come.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You'll slow us down.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Definitely. But the pup wants to come too, and I'm the only one he trusts to say yes to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The pup didn't even—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He did. He just doesn't use words.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: This is insane. We're all fishing with the dog?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Only if you want to catch anything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She's right. Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He said she was right. Marcus said Jade was *right*.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, net on that side. Marcus, you take the left cove.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The fish are running here. Morning tide.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll... create a distraction?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade, just stay away from them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That I can do.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're getting them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How are you already—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Stop talking. You're scaring them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I think the pup's doing better than me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Everyone's doing better than you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: True. I'm okay with that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Keep a fire ready. We'll need to preserve these.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus, I know. I built the—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You asked me to come. I'm helping.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I brought emotional support.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You actually did, yeah. Both of you. Thank you.

action: Leads Ryan and Jade to the tide pools at midday, reading the water like it's a book he's memorized, his movements steady and purposeful as hunger becomes a problem to solve.
thought: She asked me to teach. I said yes.

action: Wades into the shallow water behind Marcus, celebrating when their first fish is caught with breathless relief, his body suddenly warm with purpose after the empty hours.
thought: We can solve this. We're solving this.

action: Works alongside Marcus in the shallows, copying his stance, asking him to show her how to read water and shadow, her whole body alive and reaching toward him without hesitation.
thought: He's letting me reach him. I feel it.

action: Watches from the dry sand with the stray pressed against her legs, afraid to wade in but proud of them, her hand moving in slow circles on the puppy's head.
thought: They found food. I stayed back. Still.

action: Stays close to Judy's legs at the water's edge, occasionally wading a few steps and returning to her, sensing her need and choosing to anchor her to the moment.
thought: She's sad. I stay here.

description: Hunger drives them to the tide pools, where Marcus teaches Jade and Ryan to fish—and in the working moment, Jade reaches him and he doesn't pull away.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The tide's right. Food's waiting in these pools if we're quiet.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: These pools haven't been touched yet.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There—that shadow moving. Is that a fish?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fish. Definitely fish.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We'll need to move them into the shallows without spooking them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How do we do that without scaring them away?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Stone walls. Corners them slowly, gives them nowhere to go.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You've done this before?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Long time ago. Different island, different water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Different how?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just—different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, you're here now. Show us how.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I've got one cornered here!

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Where? I can't see it from here!

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Just here—can you help me drive it back?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Stay calm. Don't thrash around or you'll spook it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's working—I can feel it!

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Got it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We just caught food. Real food.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One fish. Not enough for tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But we can do this. All of us. Together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How much longer can we search before the light's gone?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe an hour. Two if we're lucky.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Will you show me how you read the water like that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. I'll show you.

action: Moves quietly beside Jade at the fire, preparing the fish with practiced hands.
thought: She reached me and I didn't pull away.

action: Tends the fire and cooks the fish, moving between them with renewed energy.
thought: Did she just look at him like that?

action: Moves close to the fire, watching Marcus work, her eyes bright with something she can't quite name.
thought: He let me stay beside him. That's everything.

action: Sits alone at the shore, watching the three of them work and laugh by the fire, her hand on her belly.
thought: They have each other. I have no one.

action: Moves between the fire and Judy's side, the pup's eyes sad, finally settling beside her in the dark.
thought: She's alone. But my warmth helps. I stay.

description: Evening finds them cooking fresh fish at the fire—Marcus and Jade glow beside the flames while Ryan tends it, and Judy watches from the shore with the stray at her side.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We actually pulled it off. Fresh fish—real food.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than the stores.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So much better. The way you showed us... I felt it click.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's got a gift for it. Something in his hands just knows the water.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You were quick to learn.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you taught me. Really taught me. Not just the how—the why.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, now I'm envious. What'd he teach you that he didn't teach me?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Different things. You think in structures. You'd build something to catch them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's right. You would. But Marcus—he reads the water like it's talking to him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That so? That's—that's something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Everyone has their way. Building's yours. Reading's mine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And mine?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You just... follow the feeling. It works.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It does. Worked today anyway. You two were quiet out there at the pools, yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He was teaching me. Showing me things I never would have seen.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She asked good questions.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade's good at that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The stray came with us too. Even the pup wanted to learn.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And Judy?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She stayed at the shore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She was tired. The pregnancy—it's wearing her down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. She's been struggling.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should make sure she eats well tonight. Make sure she knows she belongs.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Already thinking about it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We have enough now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For the first time in days. Thank you for this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's eat before it gets cold.

action: Retreats to his tent without sitting by the fire, afraid of what he's letting himself feel.
thought: I let her too close too fast.

action: Sits by the dying fire with Jade, watching Marcus pull away and sensing something has shifted between them.
thought: Something changed. I felt it today.

action: Sits by the fire beside Ryan, frustrated and confused as Marcus withdraws, trying to understand what scared him.
thought: He was open. Why is he running?

action: Stands on the dark beach, watching the firelit group from a distance, the stray her only companion.
thought: They have each other. I'm still alone.

action: Presses close to Judy on the beach, choosing her over the fire group, whimpering softly as she watches the distant flames.
thought: She's hurting. I stay.

description: At the dying fire, Marcus suddenly retreats to his tent in fear, leaving Jade reaching after him—while Ryan watches the fracture and Judy grieves alone on the beach with the stray.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You were different today. With Marcus at the tide pools.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I felt different. Like I could actually reach him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He doesn't usually let people in like that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. He was just... there. Fully there with me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Does he know what that meant to you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't think he's thinking that far ahead. He's just scared.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire's burning down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I've got it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's late.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You don't have to leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm tired.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You weren't tired ten minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That was different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's just the fire getting low. We're all exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why are you running from this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not running. I'm going to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is there fish left?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Plenty. Come sit with us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to... intrude. On whatever this is.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not intruding.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The stray's the only one who actually chooses to be with me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow. Talk tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't just shut down every time you feel something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not shutting down. I'm protecting—

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Protecting what, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Us. He's protecting us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Or himself.

action: He walks to the cliff base in the grey dawn light, needing space from what he let happen.
thought: She saw me run. Can I face her?

action: He's up early tending the fire, laying out water bowls, watching the east sky lighten.
thought: Something shifted last night. I felt it.

action: She wakes restless and walks toward the cliffs, drawn by something she can't name.
thought: He was there. Where did he go?

action: She sits at the beach's edge with the pup, watching the water, feeling the distance from last night.
thought: They have each other. I'm still alone.

action: The pup presses warm against Judy's leg, sensing her sadness and staying close.
thought: She's sad. I stay. I'm here.

description: Jade finds Marcus alone at the cliff base at dawn, and they face what shattered between them last night.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Didn't expect to see you this early.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Didn't expect you to run.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I had to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: From me?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. From how fast it was happening.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were just cooking fish, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. But you reached for me and I let you. That's when I got scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of wanting it back.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not a bad thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For me it is. I lose things.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then stop running.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Can't promise that yet.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just promise you'll come back.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm sorry about last night.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You don't have to apologize for being scared.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What about leaving?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That I didn't like. But I understand.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still figuring this out.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So am I.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We could figure it out slow.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That sounds nice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Thank you for coming to find me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Where else would I go?

action: He drifted back from the cliff as the sun climbed, settling near the house porch by midday, watching the camp breathe.
thought: I let her too close too fast.

action: He kept the fire steady through the morning, feeding it wood at intervals, watching the smoke rise and drift across the camp.
thought: Something's shifting. I can feel it.

action: She noticed Judy missing by midday and walked to the beach, found her watching the waves, took her hand, and pulled her back toward the fire.
thought: I need her to know she belongs.

action: She let Jade find her on the beach, accepted her hand, and walked back toward the fire with her, the stray padding between them both.
thought: Maybe they really do want me.

action: It stayed with Judy through the morning and walked beside her as Jade led them both back, never leaving her side.
thought: She's okay now. I made sure.

description: By midday Jade has found Judy alone at the beach and is pulling her back to camp, the stray padding alongside them, faithful.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was looking for you. You disappeared.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I just needed to get away from everything for a bit.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Away from me too?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: No. Never from you. But I was watching yesterday and I felt... left behind.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Left behind from what?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: From being part of it. You were fishing with Marcus and Ryan. The three of you were solving something together and I was just standing on the shore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy, we wanted you there. We asked.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Did you really? Or were you just being kind because you're kind to everyone?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm kind because I mean it. Sit with me.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm eight months pregnant. I can't fish. I can't swim. I can't even carry water far. What use am I?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're more than what you can do. You know that, right?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know anything anymore. I just know I'm terrified.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What are you terrified of?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: That I'll lose this. All of it. You, them, the pup. The safety I finally found.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You won't lose us. We chose you. We're still choosing you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But I'm not choosing myself. I'm just... depending on all of you to keep me here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we will. That's what we do. We keep each other here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What if I'm too much? What if carrying this baby is too much to ask of anyone?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we ask anyway. Come back with me. Come back to the fire where you belong.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know if I can face them. After pulling away like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: They won't question it. Ryan will just be glad you're there. And Marcus... Marcus understands about needing space.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: He ran last night, didn't he?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He did. And this morning we talked. Sometimes people need to run before they can stay.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is that what this is for me? Running?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe. Or maybe you just needed to be alone for a while. That's okay. But now come back. Come back with me.

action: Moves to the tide pools at low tide and finds an abundance of exposed mussels, gathering with quiet precision.
thought: They needed me. I knew what to do.

action: Arrives at the tide pools to find Marcus already working and joins him, moving carefully beside him in the shallow water.
thought: He's letting me in. We're actually okay.

action: Scouts the coves at low tide and discovers an easy pocket of fresh fish, filling her basket methodically and moving toward the group.
thought: We're stronger together than we ever were.

action: Moves to the fire with quiet purpose, preparing cooking stones and clearing space, finding her role in the afternoon's gathering.
thought: I can do something useful here.

action: Pads after Judy to the firepit and settles close, watching her work with steady presence, protective and calm.
thought: She's safe. I'm here with her.

description: At the tide pools during low tide, Ryan finds Marcus already gathering mussels; they work side by side in the shallow water, and in the quiet, Marcus finally opens up.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You found them. I wasn't sure where to start.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Low tide. They're exposed for an hour.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Can I help?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Move slow. Don't splash.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Working with you is easier somehow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: How?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're steady. I'm less anxious around you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not steady.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You are. You just hide it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're good, right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. We are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because you meant what you said about staying?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I did. I still do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Everyone could be like this if they stopped being afraid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Jade doesn't fear. And Judy—she reaches out anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back from the cliff.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You were here. That mattered.

action: He returns from the tide pools with Ryan as the sun lowers, settling by the fire with an ease that wasn't there before.
thought: I'm different here now. It feels okay.

action: He comes back from gathering mussels with Marcus, moving directly to the firepit as evening deepens, every gesture showing he's exactly where he wants to be.
thought: Marcus finally sees me. Really sees me.

action: She drifts to the firepit as darkness settles, drawn by the light and the people gathering around it.
thought: They're all showing up. Really here together.

action: She settles near the warmth of the fire, the stray curled against her side, finally not flinching at the closeness.
thought: I'm wanted. They keep coming back.

action: The pup presses close to Judy by the fire, her brown fur catching the firelight, protective and finally at rest.
thought: She's safe now. I'm here with her.

description: As evening settles, Ryan and Marcus sit by the fire together, Jade and Judy nearby with the stray; for the first time, there's room to breathe together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know, today was different. Working the tide with you—it felt right.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. It did.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all I'm getting? Just yeah?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What else is there? You know what I mean.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's saying yes completely. Marcus doesn't waste words on empty things.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I've felt like we were holding our breath around each other. Today we just... didn't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Wasn't planned. Just happened naturally.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Everything felt different today. I'm not afraid anymore. Not like I was before.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you're safe now. Truly safe.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How do you know that though? How can anyone really be sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because we chose this. All of us, choosing it together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup chose you too. Won't leave your side.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Animals know the truth before people do. She knows you matter here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: No one's ever needed me like this. Not ever.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well you're needed now. By all of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You belong here. It's not a question anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's just fact now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I want to believe that so badly.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then believe it. We're not going anywhere tomorrow or the day after.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not ever.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm going to believe it then.

action: Sits with the group at the fire as it dies, then rises and heads back to his tent, finally at peace.
thought: I'm okay. They're okay with me.

action: Gathers with everyone at the fire as it dies, then settles on the porch with Judy and the stray for the night.
thought: This is what I wanted. All of us together.

action: Leans into the warmth of the fire and the people around it, then drifts toward her hammock as night deepens.
thought: I'm not floating anymore. I've landed.

action: Sits with the group at the dying fire, the stray warm against her leg, then stays on the porch with Ryan as sleep comes.
thought: They want me here. This is real.

action: Curls tightly against Judy at the fire, then follows her to the porch where her warmth is the safest place on the island.
thought: Home is where she is. Always.

description: Around the dying firepit, four sit in easy silence as night deepens — Marcus letting go, Ryan finally at rest, Jade found, and Judy no longer trembling.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's strange, isn't it? A few days ago we were strangers.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're still kind of strangers. We're just... choosing to know each other now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I was so sure you'd all decide to leave me behind.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We didn't leave you. We went to the tide pools and came back.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we came back for you. All of us. That matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What changed? I mean, why now? Why today?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because nobody pretended anymore. We just... were.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I wanted to believe that. That it was real. I wasn't sure.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It is. Look at Marcus. He's sitting here. By choice.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And he's not counting down the minutes until he can escape.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. That part's gone now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Are you saying... you want to stay? With us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm saying I'm already here. And that feels like enough now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all any of us needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow things might be hard again. Someone will be hungry or tired.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But tonight we're all together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's the whole island right there. Tonight.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should sleep. Let the fire rest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: In the morning we'll figure out shelter, and food, and survival stuff again.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But for now I get to feel safe.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Go to bed. All of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You first, actually. You need rest like everyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Will you be okay? In your tent?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I think I will be.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Goodnight, Marcus. And... thank you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Goodnight, all of you.

action: Emerges from his tent as Ryan approaches, moving toward him without the usual hesitation.
thought: He makes this easier than I expected.

action: Greets Marcus at his tent with quiet warmth and proposes fishing at the tide pools together.
thought: He's staying. He's actually choosing this.

action: Stretches by her hammock, watches Ryan and Marcus walk toward the water, offers her help.
thought: They're finally finding their rhythm.

action: Wakes on the porch as the others move into the day, settling the pup against her.
thought: They're all okay. I'm okay too.

action: Follows Ryan and Marcus down the path toward the tide pools, tail raised and curious.
thought: Where they go, I go now.

description: Early morning: Ryan meets Marcus at his tent with quiet warmth and proposes fishing together—Marcus agrees, and the two head down the path in easy conversation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Morning. You're up early.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep much longer.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was thinking we could fish today, if you wanted. The food's running low.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tide pools? You're better at finding the shellfish.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't mind going.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Good. Let me grab the basket and we can head out.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You two are heading out?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fishing. We should be back by noon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll put water on for coffee. Some of those dried leaves from yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That sounds good.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Perfect. Keep an eye on the fire for us?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course. Go on.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How are you feeling this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Lighter than before. Like I can actually breathe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's good. Really good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still scared sometimes. Still not sure I'm doing this right.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You are. You're here, you're staying. That's everything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You make it easier. I don't know what you're doing, but it's working.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm just being here. Being honest. You're doing the hard part.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Where should we start?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Over there. I saw movement in that crevice yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll check the shallow shelf. There's usually something good there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Sounds like a plan. Let's do this.

action: Marcus returns from fishing with Ryan, his walls lower, moving among the group with less distance than before.
thought: He didn't push. Staying is easier than running.

action: Ryan guides everyone toward the catch, steady and warm, drawing all of them into the shared work.
thought: He's choosing to stay. I can see it.

action: Jade watches the change in Marcus with sudden fascination, kneeling to greet the stray while noticing his openness.
thought: Something shifted in him this morning. He's different.

action: Judy steadies on the porch with her hand on her belly, reaching to help as the group gathers.
thought: They want me here. That's real. I'm staying.

action: The stray moves between them all with certainty, pressing closest to Judy as her loyal constant.
thought: She's my safe place. Always.

description: Back from fishing with Ryan, Marcus is visibly warmer and less guarded; Jade watches the shift in him with sudden, knowing fascination.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Three good ones. Marcus spotted them in the pool—he's got a real eye.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The tide was right.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You two look different. Closer, somehow.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's staying, Jade. Really staying this time.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Easier than I thought it would be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can feel it in you. The change.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm glad. Truly. It makes me feel safer somehow.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: pads closer, tail low but present, pressing soft against Jade's leg

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You feel it too, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I want to cook them right. Help me take these somewhere quiet?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The drying rack is falling apart. We could gather wood, smoke what we catch.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A few days ago you wouldn't have planned that far. You're thinking about us now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still planning. Just differently. Not just running anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What changed, Marcus? Tell me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He didn't push me away when I let the wall down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You met me halfway. That's all I wanted.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I can help gather wood. Tell me what you need.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: rises, moves to Judy's side, tail steady, eyes on her face

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He knows she's struggling.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He knows what it feels like to be on the edge.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We all do. That's why we're still here. That's why this island isn't empty.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And you, Marcus? Why are you still here?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because staying turned out to be simpler than running.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: tail wags, rests head against Judy's leg

action: He returns from the beach with the morning's catch and, in a moment of quiet decision, invites the others to his tide pools for the first time.
thought: This is easier than I expected.

action: He comes back from fishing with Marcus, energized and already planning how they'll work together to preserve the catch properly.
thought: He's really choosing us now, really staying.

action: She watches Ryan and Marcus return from fishing, then drifts toward them, drawn by a new openness she senses in Marcus.
thought: Something shifted in him; he's letting people in.

action: She approaches with the pup at her heels, finally brave enough to ask to help with real work rather than hover on the edges.
thought: I belong here, they want me here.

action: It presses close to Judy as they walk toward the tide pools, sensing her newfound confidence and mirroring her ease.
thought: She's safe; I'm safe; we're all here.

description: Marcus brings Ryan, Jade, and Judy to the tide pools to clean the catch—his private place, finally shared, finally not his alone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We've got a good catch here. What do you think—cook most of it tonight and preserve the rest?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah, that works. We need the fire hot enough for both.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're better with fire than I am. I always push it too hard.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't push. You're just eager. It's different from pushing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There's something different about you two right now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We had a good morning. Fishing was easy with him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It was easy because he was there. Because we were working together without the walls between us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're actually letting him help instead of pushing him away.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He's making it easier to stay. Easier than I thought it could be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can see that. It's really good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It is good. I didn't think I could feel like this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You can. You are. And that matters.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What are you three doing? Can I help with the fish? The pup wants to help too.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're taking them to the tide pools to clean them properly. You should come.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I want to. But isn't that your private spot, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It was. But it doesn't have to be just mine anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So we're all going? All of us together?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. All of us. That's what I want.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's head there before the light changes. We'll make quick work of it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I like being part of this. Being wanted.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You are wanted. We all know it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Thank you. For bringing me in.

action: He let Ryan help steady his fears in the fading light, then gathered firewood together, finally choosing to stay by the fire.
thought: He's not leaving. Maybe I don't have to run.

action: He found Marcus alone at dusk, sat with him and promised to stay, then gathered firewood as the light faded.
thought: He finally let me in. This is real.

action: She watched from the firelight as Ryan and Marcus gathered wood together, then quietly affirmed Marcus when they returned.
thought: He's staying. We all are staying now.

action: She tended the fire by the house porch, made tea from fresh leaves, and welcomed everyone back to warmth.
thought: They all came back to the fire, to me.

action: It settled against Marcus as evening fell, offering silent support, staying at his side by the fire with quiet devotion.
thought: He's mine and I'm his. We stay.

description: In the fading light, Ryan gently steadied Marcus's fears while gathering firewood; by evening, all four sat at the fire with the stray nestled against Marcus, the group finally whole and grounded.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You've been quiet all evening. What's going on?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just thinking.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: About this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About what happens after. When you get tired of me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What if we don't?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: People always do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not going anywhere. I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Until you're not.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're scared.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.
